content:
First place the rectangle so that  
A=(0,0), B=(5,0), D=(0,12), C=(5,12).  
Since E is 12 units from AB (the x–axis) and 1 unit from AD (the y–axis), and △ABE must be acute and E must lie “outside” (i.e. below the rectangle), one finds  
E=(1,−12).  

Any line through E meeting AB at F=(t,0) and CD (the line y=12) at G will satisfy  
– slope = (0−(−12))/(t−1)=12/(t−1),  
– plugging y=12 gives Gₓ=1+2(t−1)=2t−1, so G=(2t−1,12).  

This line chops the 5×12 rectangle (area 60) into two polygons.  The “left” piece A–F–G–D has area (by the shoelace formula)  
½·|(0·0 + t·12 + (2t−1)·12 + 0·0) − 0|  
=½·(36t−12)  
=18t−6.  
Setting this equal to half the rectangle, 30, gives 18t−6=30 ⇒ t=2.  

Hence  
F=(2,0),  G=(2·2−1,12)=(3,12),  
so  
FG²=(3−2)²+(12−0)²=1+144=145.  

\boxed{145}

\boxed{145}
